name: Sabbatic Craft
description: The Sabbatic Craft, also known as Traditional Witchcraft, represents a diverse collection of magical practices and beliefs rooted in pre-Christian European folklore, folk magic, and encounters with spirits. Unlike modern Wicca, it emphasizes direct interaction with spirits of the land, the Wild Hunt, and powerful figures associated with witchcraft lore, often identified with the Devil in a complex and symbolic manner. Its origins are obscure, drawing from rural traditions, cunning folk practices, and heretical beliefs, evolving over centuries. Sabbatic Craft involves altered states of consciousness achieved through ritual, trance, herbalism, and ecstatic practices to commune with spirits, gain knowledge, and perform magic. Key elements include the Witches' Sabbath, a gathering where practitioners meet in spirit or physically to celebrate, share knowledge, and engage in magical workings. Emphasis is placed on personal gnosis, experience, and a deep connection to the natural world, often rejecting dogma and embracing a more individualistic approach to witchcraft. The cultural significance lies in its exploration of marginalized beliefs, resistance to dominant religious structures, and a celebration of the wild, untamed aspects of nature and the human spirit. Practitioners often work with familiar spirits, engage in hedge riding (soul flight), and cultivate a relationship with the 'Otherworld'.
subtitle: Old Ways, Spirit Flight, Ecstatic Witchcraft

keyMyths.title: The Witches' Sabbath
keyMyths.description: Witches gather in secret at the Sabbath, a place of revelry and magical power, where they meet with the Devil and perform rituals. This meeting is often depicted as a wild and ecstatic event, central to the Craft.

keyMyths.title: Flight on the Besom
keyMyths.description: Witches are said to fly on their besoms, traveling to the Sabbath or other magical locations. This flight symbolizes the ability to transcend the mundane and enter the spirit realm, often through altered states of consciousness.
keyMyths.source: European folklore and witch trial accounts

sources.author: Emma Wilby
sources.work: Cunning Folk and Familiar Spirits: Shamanistic Visionary Traditions in Early Modern British Witchcraft
sources.description: Explores the shamanistic elements within early modern witchcraft beliefs.
sources.text: Explores the shamanistic elements within early modern witchcraft beliefs.

sources.author: Nigel Pennick
sources.work: Pagan Magic of the Northern Tradition
sources.description: Examines the pre-Christian roots of Northern European magical practices.
sources.text: Examines the pre-Christian roots of Northern European magical practices.
